hero.title: quantitative portfolio rebalancer
hero.subtitle: No emotions. No human error. Structural limits on every DCA dollar you deploy.
hero.description: AQMath processes complex covariance matrices on our secure backend engine. Your holdings are processed in-memory and never stored on our servers — only your browser's localStorage keeps your data.
hero.follow: follow for updates & news: [ x ] · [ telegram ]

math.heading: The Math Behind Your Portfolio
math.lead: The covariance matrix runs on our secure backend. All computation is stateless — data is processed in-memory and discarded immediately. Nothing is written to disk.
math.rule1: High-variance assets receive smaller dynamic caps
math.rule2: Correlated assets are penalized through covariance
math.rule3: Underweight assets are prioritized only when they improve the risk-balanced structure
math.riskCopy: The covariance matrix estimates how assets move together. Risk Parity then targets equal risk contribution — so no single token dominates your volatility.
math.optCopy: KKT projection keeps allocations inside volatility caps and prevents oversized high-risk positions. It parks new DCA capital in USDC when drawdown is severe — stops you buying into a crash.
math.privCopy: Amounts, targets, and allocation weights stay in your browser's localStorage. Data sent to the backend is processed in-memory and discarded immediately — nothing is persisted.
math.shieldCopy: Emergency Brake: on crash detection, exposure drops via 3-stage sell-down to 0% (full stablecoins) — DCA locked via Circuit Breaker. Capital re-enters via 3-stage tranche recovery when the Shield returns to NORMAL.

what.title: What is AQMath, How It Works & Why
what.lead: A privacy-first quantitative rebalancer for passive crypto investors.
what.whatP:
AQMath is a quantitative portfolio rebalancer built for passive crypto investors.

It combines institutional-grade mathematics — Risk Parity, Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) optimization, and volatility-weighted Dollar-Cost Averaging — to keep your portfolio balanced without emotion, guesswork, or centralized data collection.

Non-Custodial Math Processing. Your portfolio data is sent only during DCA/Optimize and never persisted. No accounts. No tracking. Just math.
what.howP:
1. Add your tokens and target allocations (or let the engine calculate them using 180-day historical volatility).

2. Click SYNC to get live prices from Binance — price data is fetched directly from Binance via WebSocket.

3. Use DCA Distribution to invest new capital only into underweight tokens, filtered by volatility and trend.

4. (Pro) Run the Quantum Engine to re-compute optimal weights using full covariance matrix and KKT projection.
what.whyP:
Most portfolio tools either collect your data or offer only basic rebalancing. AQMath is different:

→ Institutional-grade math (same concepts used by multi-billion funds)
→ Non-Custodial — your portfolio stays in your browser’s localStorage, backend processing is ephemeral
→ Transparent, auditable logic (no black boxes)
→ Exclusive annual access — selection-based, processed via USDC & EURC

Your balances are ephemeral — processed in-memory, never stored. No exchange API keys required. No data to sell.

faq.title: --frequently-asked-questions
faq.q1: How does the engine calculate risk?
faq.a1: The Black engine uses a Variance Matrix, asset Covariance, volatility estimates, and KKT-style projection to calculate Risk Parity allocations where each asset contributes a controlled share of portfolio risk. You can audit the full mathematical model in our [Documentation].
faq.a2: We don't track your wallet and we don't have a user database. Your token balances are stored only in your browser's localStorage. During DCA and Optimize, position data is sent to our backend, processed in-memory, and discarded immediately — nothing is persisted. Your financial privacy is architecturally enforced. Read the full privacy architecture in our [Documentation].
faq.q3: What's included in Free vs Black?
faq.a3: The free version is fully functional for manual portfolio tracking, CSV-based historical prices, DCA distribution, and browser-local export/import. Black adds automated historical data pipeline, full Risk Parity optimization, Deleverage Shield crash protection, and unlimited token support.
faq.q4: Why are market widgets separated from features?
faq.a4: Live markets are informational context: ticker data, Fear & Greed, BTC dominance, movers, and exchange status. They support decision-making but are intentionally separate from the product feature story.
faq.a5: When enabled, USDC acts as a stablecoin buffer inside your portfolio. Any surplus from hard caps or unallocated DCA budget flows into USDC instead of being lost. Toggle it OFF to treat USDC as a normal token.
faq.q6: How do I apply for Black access?
faq.a6: DM us on Telegram or X. Access is granted manually after a brief screening. Annual pricing is disclosed upon approval. Payments accepted exclusively in USDC and EURC.
faq.a7: No. AQMath operates exclusively on an annual subscription model. We do not offer monthly, quarterly, or semi-annual plans. All sales are final — no refunds.
faq.a8: No. AQMath is a premium digital assistant and analytical web application. We do not execute trades, manage funds, or provide software that interacts with crypto exchanges or smart contracts.
faq.a9: No. AQMath is a highly optimized web application accessible via compatible browsers. We prioritize infrastructure quality and security over mass market deployment. No iOS or Android app is available.

disclaimer:
AQMath does not provide financial advice. All calculations are based on historical data and mathematical models.
Mathematical processing runs on our secure backend; your portfolio data is processed in-memory and never persisted.
© 2026 AQMath — Secure Backend Engine. Private. Non-Custodial.
Documentation · Impressum · Privacy & Legacy Policy · Terms · Follow: X (Twitter)

faq.a1: A non-custodial tool for long-term passive crypto portfolio management using DCA and quantitative optimization (Risk Parity).
faq.a2: You enter a total budget, and the app allocates it to tokens below their target allocation, considering risk and average cost.
faq.q3: What does the Quantum Engine do?
faq.a3: It uses 180 days of price history to compute optimal allocation weights (Risk Parity) so each token contributes equally to portfolio risk.
faq.a4: You can manually enter, import a CSV with transactions (Date, Type, Symbol, Amount) or load a previously exported JSON.
faq.a6: The token has less than 180 days of price history – optimization results are less reliable.
faq.a7: When enabled, USDC acts as a stablecoin buffer. Excess funds from hard caps and unallocated DCA budget flow into USDC instead of being lost. Toggle it OFF to treat USDC as a normal token.
faq.a8: Your portfolio amounts, targets, and allocation choices are stored in your browser (localStorage). During DCA and Optimize, positions are sent to our backend, processed in-memory, and discarded immediately. Non-custodial by design.

app.footerDisclaimer: AQMath does not provide financial advice.

doc.title: System Documentation
doc.subtitle: AQMath Quantitative Portfolio Rebalancer — Technical Reference

s1.title: 1. What is AQMath?
s1.p1: AQMath is a quantitative portfolio rebalancer purpose-built for the crypto market. It combines institutional-grade mathematical models — the same class of optimization used by multi-billion-dollar hedge funds — with a privacy-first architecture that ensures your financial data never leaves your control.
s1.p2: At its core, AQMath solves a deceptively complex problem: when you add new capital to your crypto portfolio, which tokens should you buy, and how much of each? A naive approach (split equally) ignores that some positions are already overweight while others are dangerously underweight. AQMath’s DCA engine uses smart proportional allocation, filtered through a DCA safety pipeline, to ensure every dollar you invest moves your portfolio closer to its optimal structure.
s1.p3: The system operates on a hybrid architecture: your portfolio data (token amounts, entry prices, targets) is stored exclusively in your browser’s localStorage, while computationally intensive math (covariance matrices, iterative DCA optimization, volatility analysis) runs on our secure backend engine. This means you get institutional-quality analysis without your holdings being stored on any server.
s1.privP: AQMath’s privacy model is non-custodial by design: the backend receives position data in-memory during a single DCA request, computes the result, and discards everything. No persistence. No logs of your amounts. No user database. Your financial state lives exclusively in your browser’s localStorage — the server never retains it.
s1.whoP: AQMath is designed for a specific type of crypto investor:
s1.who1: Passive DCA investors who regularly add capital and want to know the mathematically optimal way to distribute it across their portfolio — not just “buy more BTC” but “buy more of whatever is most underweight right now.”
s1.who2: Long-term holders who believe in their token thesis (6–18+ months) but want to avoid emotional rebalancing decisions driven by market hype or fear.
s1.who3: Privacy-conscious users who refuse to connect wallets, hand over API keys, or trust a centralized service with their portfolio data. AQMath requires zero accounts, zero wallet connections, and zero data sharing.
s1.who4: DeFi participants who hold tokens across multiple chains and wallets and need a unified view with quantitative allocation — without giving any single service visibility into their total exposure.
s1.who5: Risk-aware allocators who understand that a 30% position in a single altcoin is not “diversified” just because it has a high target — and want structural limits (hard caps, risk budgets, circuit breakers) enforced automatically.
s1.notBot: AQMath is not a trading bot. It does not execute trades, predict prices, or time the market. It is a mathematical allocation engine that tells you exactly how to distribute new capital for maximum portfolio balance — and then gets out of your way.

s2.title: 2. System Architecture
s2.p1: AQMath uses a hybrid architecture that separates data ownership from computational power. Your browser owns the data; the backend owns the math. This is fundamentally different from traditional portfolio tools that store everything on centralized servers, creating a single point of failure and a tempting target for data breaches.
s2.dfP: Understanding the data flow is critical to understanding AQMath’s privacy guarantees. Every interaction follows the same pattern: your browser sends the minimum information needed for computation, and the backend returns results without retaining any state.
s2.df1: User enters tokens + targets → stored exclusively in browser localStorage. No network request is made.
s2.df2: SYNC clicked → prices are fetched directly from Binance via WebSocket — no backend call needed. Your holdings are never transmitted.
s2.df3: DCA Distribute clicked → positions (including amounts) and DCA budget are sent to the backend engine via a single HTTPS POST request.
s2.df4: Backend computes → the DCA engine runs iterative allocation with the full safety pipeline. All computation happens in-memory. Nothing is written to disk or database.
s2.df5: Results returned → updated token amounts are sent back to the browser and written to localStorage. The backend discards the request data immediately after responding.
s2.cleanP: Before price data reaches the optimization engine, it passes through a multi-stage cleaning pipeline that ensures mathematical integrity. Raw market data from multiple sources (CoinGecko, Kraken, Coinbase) is often noisy — containing duplicates, exchange-specific outliers, and occasional gaps. The cleaning pipeline applies four sequential filters:
s2.clean1: Deduplication — removes duplicate timestamps, keeping the latest entry per date.
s2.clean2: Outlier Removal — rejects prices that deviate more than 2 standard deviations from a 7-day rolling window, eliminating flash-crash spikes and stale quotes.
s2.clean3: Gap Interpolation — fills gaps of 2 days or fewer using linear interpolation, preserving trend continuity.
s2.clean4: Multi-Source Merge — when the same token is collected from multiple exchanges, the median price is used to eliminate exchange-specific bias.
s2.cleanP2: This pipeline runs daily at 01:00 UTC via a scheduled cron job. The result is a clean, continuous price series suitable for covariance and volatility calculations.
s2.zkP: AQMath’s privacy architecture is non-custodial by design. The backend processes your DCA request statelessly — it receives position data, computes optimal allocation, returns results, and discards everything from memory. No persistent record of your holdings is ever created server-side:
s2.zk1: No user database exists — there is nothing to hack, leak, or subpoena
s2.zk2: No portfolio amounts are logged or stored server-side after a DCA response
s2.zk3: No wallet addresses, API keys, or identity information is ever collected
s2.zk4: The backend cannot reconstruct your portfolio even if it wanted to — it simply doesn’t retain the data
s2.zk5: Your browser’s localStorage is the single source of truth for your financial state
s2.zkWhyP: In 2023–2025, multiple centralized portfolio trackers suffered data breaches exposing user holdings. With AQMath, a server breach would reveal nothing about your portfolio — because there is nothing to find. Your financial sovereignty is guaranteed by architecture, not by promises.

s3.title: 3. Portfolio Tracking
s3.p1: Your portfolio is a collection of token positions stored entirely in your browser’s localStorage. For each token, AQMath tracks your holdings, target allocation percentage, current price, staking yield, entry price history, and DCA cost basis. Together, these parameters form the complete state of your portfolio and drive every calculation in the system — from allocation metrics to risk budget enforcement.
s3.pmP1: Every time the UI renders or a DCA calculation runs, the system computes real-time metrics for each token. It measures how much each position is currently worth, what share of your total portfolio it represents, and how far it has drifted from your intended target.
s3.pmP2: The DCA engine uses delta — the dollar gap between where a token is and where it should be — to prioritize which tokens receive new capital first. Tokens that are most underweight relative to their target get funded first. Safe-haven tokens (stablecoins) bypass this filter entirely and always receive capital when designated.
s3.pnlP: Profit and loss is tracked against your cost basis — the true dollar-cost-averaged entry price across all purchases. As the DCA engine buys more tokens over time, it accumulates your cost basis and total tokens acquired, giving you an accurate average price and return percentage for each position.

s4.title: 4. DCA Distribution Engine
s4.p1: The DCA engine is the heart of AQMath. Given a budget (e.g., $500), it determines exactly how to distribute that capital across your portfolio’s underweight tokens — not equally, not randomly, but proportionally to how far each token is from its target, adjusted for risk.
s4.p2: The engine uses an iterative allocation algorithm that converges over multiple rounds. In each round, it distributes the remaining budget to the most underweight tokens, removes tokens that have reached their target, and redistributes the freed-up share to remaining candidates. This process repeats until the entire budget is allocated or all eligible tokens are fulfilled.
s4.howP1: Before any allocation happens, each token is evaluated through the DCA safety pipeline (see Section 5). Only tokens that pass all filters are eligible to receive DCA funds. The allocation then follows a simple principle: the more underweight a token is relative to its target, the larger share of new capital it receives — tempered by a risk adjustment that reduces allocation for highly volatile assets.
s4.howP2: After each round, cost basis and token counts are updated automatically. Over multiple DCA cycles, the engine naturally balances your portfolio toward its ideal allocation.
s4.smallP: When the DCA budget is very small, the engine allocates the entire amount to the single most underweight eligible token.
s4.smallWhyP: If you DCA $20 across 5 tokens, each gets $4 — likely below exchange minimums, and fees would consume a significant percentage. The small DCA rule puts the full $20 into the token that needs it most. Over multiple DCA cycles, the algorithm naturally balances across all tokens.

s5.title: 5. DCA Safety Pipeline
s5.p1: AQMath implements a multi-layer safety pipeline for DCA allocation designed for crypto markets. Traditional systems would halt trading on a 15% dip — but in crypto, 15–20% pullbacks happen regularly and are often excellent buying opportunities. Each layer operates independently and in strict sequence. Note: This pipeline governs only how new DCA capital is allocated — it does not trim or sell existing positions.
s5.l1P: The circuit breaker halts all DCA distribution only when the Deleverage Shield detects an extreme market crash (flash crash or volatility spike). It acts as a slave to the Shield — blocking DCA during emergency events and automatically resuming when the Shield returns to NORMAL. Unlike traditional drawdown-based breakers, it never blocks DCA during extended bear markets.
s5.l2P: A per-token risk adjustment that reduces buy size for high-volatility assets. More volatile tokens receive proportionally less capital, ensuring that risky positions don’t get outsized allocations even when they’re the most underweight.
s5.l3P: Skips tokens currently trading above their moving average — effectively enforcing a “buy the dip” discipline. Capital flows to tokens during pullbacks, not during rallies.
s5.l4P: Tokens earning high staking yields compound on their own and are excluded from DCA distribution. The engine redirects that capital to lower-yield tokens that need active injection.
s5.l5P: No single risky token can exceed a structural concentration limit. If allocation would push a token beyond this cap, the excess is trimmed and redirected to the surplus pool.
s5.l5ProP: The Black engine replaces the flat cap with volatility-adaptive limits: high-volatility tokens receive smaller caps (as low as 10%), while low-volatility tokens can hold larger positions (up to 40%). Each token’s maximum weight is dynamically scaled between 10% and 40% based on how volatile it is relative to the most volatile token in the portfolio. The more volatile a token, the tighter its cap.
s5.l6P: Total risky exposure is capped at a percentage of your portfolio, ensuring a safety buffer always exists. If risky tokens exceed the budget, all allocations are scaled down proportionally.
s5.l7P: All surplus capital from hard caps and risk budget scaling flows into your designated stablecoin. This capital sits in a safe buffer until you decide to redeploy it through DCA.

s6.title: 6. Volatility Calculation
s6.p1: Volatility is the foundational risk metric used throughout AQMath. It feeds into the safety factor, the Deleverage Shield's downside detection, and (in Black) the covariance matrix. The system computes a rolling volatility estimate for each token by measuring the magnitude of daily price swings — a standard approach in quantitative finance.
s6.pvP: Portfolio-level volatility is a weighted aggregate of each token’s individual volatility, proportional to its share of total portfolio value. Safe-haven tokens and frozen positions are excluded since they have near-zero volatility by definition. This portfolio-level metric drives adaptive thresholds across the entire safety pipeline.
s6.covP: The Black engine computes a full covariance matrix estimating how assets move together. Two tokens with moderate individual volatility but high correlation (like ETH and SOL) have amplified combined risk. The optimizer uses this to ensure each asset contributes equal risk — preventing hidden concentration through correlation.

s8.title: 8. AQMath Engine (Black)
s8.p1: The AQMath Engine is the Black-tier optimization layer that sits alongside the DCA engine. While the DCA engine distributes a fixed dollar budget, the AQMath Engine answers a deeper question: what should the target weights themselves be? It uses institutional-grade quantitative methods — the same class of algorithms deployed by multi-billion-dollar hedge funds — to compute mathematically optimal portfolio weights from first principles.
s8.p2: The engine runs a three-stage pipeline: it builds a full covariance matrix from 180 days of cleaned price history, solves for Equal Risk Contribution (true Risk Parity), and applies KKT projection with dynamic volatility caps to enforce structural limits.
s8.ercP1: Traditional portfolio optimization (Markowitz mean-variance) requires estimating expected returns — a notoriously unreliable process that amplifies estimation errors into extreme allocations. Risk Parity sidesteps this entirely by optimizing for equal risk contribution rather than equal weight or maximum return.
s8.ercP2: The ERC solver uses a fixed-point iteration algorithm that converges to weights where each asset contributes exactly an equal share of total portfolio risk. The process works as follows:
s8.erc1: Each token’s risk contribution is calculated by multiplying its weight by its marginal contribution to overall portfolio volatility.
s8.erc2: The solver repeatedly adjusts weights — increasing allocation to tokens that contribute too little risk, decreasing those that contribute too much.
s8.erc3: The algorithm typically converges to a stable solution within 5–15 iterations.
s8.ercP3: The result is a portfolio where BTC and a small-cap altcoin each contribute the same amount of risk — preventing any single asset from dominating your portfolio’s volatility profile, even if it has the largest allocation.
s8.kktP: The raw ERC weights may violate real-world constraints (e.g., a 35% allocation to a single token). The KKT projection step enforces structural limits through iterative clipping:
s8.kkt1: Dynamic per-token caps — each token’s maximum weight is computed from its volatility. The more volatile a token is compared to the rest of the portfolio, the tighter its cap (as low as 10%). Stable, low-volatility tokens can hold up to 40%.
s8.kkt2: Risk budget — total risky exposure is capped at 60%. If ERC weights exceed this, all allocations are scaled down proportionally.
s8.kkt3: Minimum floor — tokens below 1% are removed, preventing dust allocations.
s8.kkt4: Surplus redistribution — capital freed by capping is redistributed to uncapped tokens proportionally.
s8.kktP2: The projection runs iteratively (up to 10 rounds) until all constraints are simultaneously satisfied.
s8.corrP1: The engine computes a full NxN covariance matrix from 180 days of cleaned, aligned price history. This matrix captures not just individual asset volatility but how assets move together. Two tokens with moderate individual volatility but high correlation (e.g., ETH and SOL tend to move in the same direction 85% of the time) have amplified combined risk — a relationship that simple weight-based allocation ignores entirely.
s8.corr1: Covariance matrix — upper-triangle annualized covariances for all token pairs
s8.corr2: Top correlations — the 6 highest-correlated pairs, sorted by correlation strength
s8.corr3: Portfolio volatility — the aggregate annualized volatility after KKT projection
s8.corr4: Per-token diagnostics — ERC weight, KKT weight, risk contribution, volatility, dynamic cap, and capped status
s8.rpWhyP: A naïve equal-weight portfolio of BTC + SOL + DOGE allocates 33% each. But DOGE’s volatility is roughly 3x BTC’s, meaning DOGE contributes ~60% of total portfolio risk despite holding only 33% of value. ERC automatically corrects this: it would allocate ~55% to BTC, ~25% to SOL, and ~20% to DOGE — ensuring each token genuinely contributes one-third of the risk.

s9.title: 9. Privacy & Data Separation
s9.p1: AQMath’s privacy is not a feature bolted onto the product — it’s the foundational architectural principle. The system is designed so that your financial data is mathematically isolated from the computation layer.
s9.bnP: This is the most critical section. The following data is architecturally impossible for the backend to access:
s9.bnCtP: No database table maps users to portfolios. No log file records your holdings. Even with full server access, an attacker would find zero information about any user’s financial state.
s9.t1b: Never entered — AQMath doesn’t connect to wallets
s9.t2b: Never requested — price sync uses public endpoints
s9.t3b: Never transmitted — only current state during DCA
s9.t4b: No accounts, no email, no registration
s9.t5b: Sent during DCA but never persisted
s9.t7b: Sent during DCA for avg calc but not stored
s9.t8b: JWT validated but not linked to portfolio data
s9.bnP2: The backend processes your DCA request in a single HTTP round-trip: receive positions → compute allocation → return results → discard. There is no database table that maps users to portfolios. There is no log file that records your holdings. Even if someone gained full access to the server, they would find zero information about any user’s financial state.
s9.zkP: AQMath implements a practical non-custodial privacy model:
s9.zk1: The backend proves that the DCA allocation is mathematically optimal through detailed buy summaries (e.g., “BTC: +$142.30 (0.002371 tokens)”).
s9.zk2: The backend does not retain the inputs. After the HTTP response is sent, the request data is garbage-collected from memory. No disk write. No database insert.
s9.zk3: The browser verifies the results by applying updated amounts and re-rendering the portfolio.
s9.zkP2: The backend does not retain your data after the HTTP response is sent — request data is garbage-collected from memory with no disk write, no database insert, and no logs of your holdings. Your portfolio lives in your browser’s localStorage, and nowhere else.
s9.compP: Traditional trackers store everything on centralized servers — one breach exposes all users. AQMath stores nothing server-side. No accounts, no wallet connections, no analytics tracking. Your portfolio lives in your browser, and nowhere else.
s9.closing: AQMath gives you the computational power of a quantitative hedge fund’s allocation engine while maintaining the data sovereignty of a paper notebook.

bt.intro: How it works: Upload historical price CSVs for your tokens (up to 5). The engine runs the exact same deleverage state machine (v10.6) used in production. Both strategies receive identical DCA capital on the same schedule for a methodologically fair comparison. Buy & Hold buys tokens unconditionally. Deleverage parks DCA in USDC when the shield is ON, then deploys via tranches: T1 (25% when vol calms), T2 (50% when gap <15%), Final (100% on shield exit).
bt.csvFormat: CSV format: Header with date column (event_date/date/Date) + price column (close_price_usd/Close/price). Equal-weighted portfolio.

terms.title: 📄 Terms of Service & Disclaimer
terms.intro: Welcome to aqmath.xyz (the "Website"). By accessing our Website, using our Web Application, or applying for our services, you agree to comply with and be bound by the following Terms of Service.
terms.s1: AQMATH operates strictly on an elite, selection-based Annual Subscription model. We do not offer monthly, quarterly, or semi-annual plans. Access is granted manually to approved users. Due to the immediate delivery of digital access, data insights, and the highly exclusive nature of this digital assistant service, ALL SALES ARE FINAL. We operate under a strict NO REFUND policy. By making an annual payment via USDC or EURC, you explicitly acknowledge and agree that you waive any right to a refund, partial refund, or chargeback.
terms.s2: Your approved subscription grants you authorization to access the AQMATH Web Application via compatible web browsers. AQMATH DOES NOT offer, support, or distribute a mobile application (No iOS/Android App). Any mobile application using our name on any app store is unauthorized, fraudulent, and not affiliated with us.
terms.s3Title: 3. Not a Trading Bot / No Automated Execution
terms.s3: AQMATH is a premium "White Glove" digital assistant and analytical Web Application. AQMATH IS NOT A TRADING BOT, automated trading system, or algorithmic trade execution software. We do not manage funds, execute trades on behalf of users, or provide software that interacts with crypto exchanges or smart contracts.
terms.s4: To maintain maximum infrastructure performance and service quality, access to the AQMATH Web App is strictly throttled and limited to a selective user base (Quality over Quantity). We reserve the absolute right to deny access, reject applications, or terminate service for any user at our sole discretion, without providing prior notice, to safeguard our network integrity.
terms.s5: Official annual subscription pricing is confidential and only disclosed to users who successfully pass our screening process. All official payments are accepted and processed exclusively in USDC and EURC stablecoins.
terms.s6: The tools, data, and assistance provided by the AQMATH Web App are for informational and educational purposes only. We DO NOT provide financial advice, investment advice, or capital management. Cryptocurrencies and Web3 markets involve extreme volatility and high risk. You are solely responsible for your financial decisions and any capital losses incurred.

impressum.enLinks: This website may contain links to external websites of third parties, over whose content I have no influence. Therefore, I cannot assume any liability for this external content. The respective provider or operator of the pages is always responsible for the content of the linked pages. The linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognizable at the time of linking. However, permanent monitoring of the content of the linked pages is not reasonable without concrete evidence of a violation of the law. Upon notification of violations, I will remove such links immediately.
impressum.enCopy: The content and works created by me on these pages are subject to German copyright law. Duplication, processing, distribution, or any form of commercialization of such material beyond the scope of copyright law requires my prior written consent. Downloads and copies of this site are only permitted for private, non-commercial use.

privacy.title: 📄 Datenschutzerklärung / Privacy & Legacy Policy
privacy.deS1: Diese Website verfolgt einen strengen „Privacy-by-Design“-Ansatz. Es gibt keine Benutzerkonten, keine Wallets und keine serverseitigen Datenbanken. Wir speichern, protokollieren oder persistieren Ihre persönlichen Portfolio- oder Finanzdaten zu keinem Zeitpunkt auf unserer Infrastruktur.
privacy.deS2: Wenn Sie Berechnungen auf unserer Website durchführen, werden die von Ihnen eingegebenen Daten (wie Token-Mengen oder Symbole) an unseren Server übertragen, um die mathematischen Operationen auszuführen.
privacy.deS2Li1: Keine Speicherung: Die Verarbeitung erfolgt ausschließlich im flüchtigen Arbeitsspeicher (RAM) des Servers.
privacy.deS2Li2: Sofortige Löschung: Sobald die Berechnung abgeschlossen und das Ergebnis an Ihren Browser zurückgesendet wurde, werden alle Eingabedaten sofort und unwiderruflich aus dem Arbeitsspeicher gelöscht.
privacy.deS2Li3: Keine Protokolle: Es erfolgt keine Speicherung in Datenbanken, auf Festplatten oder in Server-Logdateien.
privacy.deS3: Wir nutzen zur datenschutzfreundlichen Analyse unserer Website den Dienst Simple Analytics (Simple Analytics BV, Niederlande). Dieser Dienst wird in der kostenfreien Variante (Free Plan) betrieben. Simple Analytics verwendet keine Cookies, speichert nichts im LocalStorage Ihres Browsers und erfasst keine IP-Adressen oder personenbezogenen Daten. Es werden ausschließlich rein anonyme und aggregierte Metriken erhoben, um zu sehen, aus welchen Ländern unsere Besucher kommen und welche Geräte/Browser genutzt werden. Ein Rückschluss auf einzelne Personen ist absolut unmöglich. Da keine personenbezogenen Daten verarbeitet werden, ist hierfür kein Cookie-Banner erforderlich.
privacy.deS4: Unsere digitale Infrastruktur ist wie folgt aufgeteilt:
privacy.deS4Li1: Frontend-Hosting (GitHub Pages): Die statischen Dateien der Website werden über GitHub Pages bereitgestellt (GitHub, Inc., USA). GitHub erhebt automatisch Server-Logfiles (IP-Adresse, Browsertyp, Uhrzeit), um die Sicherheit und Stabilität des Dienstes zu gewährleisten (Rechtsgrundlage: Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. f DSGVO).
privacy.deS4Li2: Backend-Server (Railway): Unser Berechnungsserver wird von Railway (Railway Corp., USA) auf Servern in Singapur gehostet. Bei der Übertragung Ihrer Anfrage werden Ihre IP-Adresse und die Berechnungsdaten kurzzeitig im RAM verarbeitet. Da Singapur ein Drittland außerhalb der EU ist, erfolgt die Datenübertragung auf Grundlage der Standardvertragsklauseln (SCC) des Infrastrukturanbieters, um ein angemessenes Datenschutzniveau zu gewährleisten (Rechtsgrundlage: Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. b DSGVO – Vertragserfüllung).
privacy.deS5: Um Ihre Portfolio-Einstellungen und Präferenzen lokal zwischen den Sitzungen zu sichern, nutzt diese Website den „localStorage“ Ihres Webbrowsers. Diese Daten verbleiben ausschließlich auf Ihrem eigenen Gerät. Da diese Speicherung technisch notwendig ist, um die von Ihnen gewünschte Funktion bereitzustellen, ist sie nach § 25 Abs. 2 TDDDG (ehemals TTDSG) ohne explizite Einwilligung zulässig. Sie können diese Daten jederzeit selbst löschen, indem Sie Ihren Browser-Cache leeren.
privacy.deS6: Zur Anzeige von Echtzeitkursen ruft Ihr Browser Kursdaten direkt von öffentlichen APIs (z. B. Binance) ab. Hierbei wird Ihre IP-Adresse vom jeweiligen API-Anbieter verarbeitet, um die Anfrage zu beantworten. Es werden keine Portfolio-Daten an diese APIs gesendet.
privacy.deS7P1: Haftungsausschluss: Dieser Dienst wird „wie besehen“ (as is) zur Verfügung gestellt. Alle finanziellen Entscheidungen liegen in der alleinigen Verantwortung des Nutzers. Es wird keine Haftung für Verluste oder Schäden übernommen, die aus der Nutzung dieses Dienstes resultieren.
privacy.deS7P2: Legacy Policy: Im Falle einer künftigen Einstellung dieses Dienstes verbleiben keinerlei Benutzerdaten auf unseren Systemen, da serverseitig nichts gespeichert wird. Die im LocalStorage gesicherten Daten verbleiben auf Ihrem eigenen Gerät und können von Ihnen jederzeit gelöscht werden.
privacy.deS8: Da wir keine personenbezogenen Daten oder Portfolio-Daten auf unseren Servern speichern, können wir Ihnen keine Auskunft erteilen oder Daten löschen – wir besitzen sie schlichtweg nicht. Bei Fragen zur flüchtigen Verarbeitung Ihrer Verbindungsdaten können Sie uns unter momo.demirov@gmail.com kontaktieren.
privacy.enS1: This service is designed with privacy-first principles: no user accounts, no wallets, and no server-side databases. We do not store, log, or persist your financial or portfolio data on our infrastructure.
privacy.enS2: When you use our computation features, the data you enter (such as token amounts or symbols) is transmitted to our backend server to perform the necessary math operations.
privacy.enS2Li1: Zero Retention: This data is processed strictly in-memory (RAM) on our server.
privacy.enS2Li2: Immediate Deletion: As soon as the computation is finished and the result is sent back to your browser, all input data is immediately and permanently discarded from the server’s memory.
privacy.enS2Li3: No Logs: We do not write your portfolio data to any database, hard drive, or server log files.
privacy.enS3: To understand our traffic patterns without invading your privacy, we use Simple Analytics (Simple Analytics BV, Netherlands) on their Free Plan. This tool does not use cookies, does not use local storage, and does not collect IP addresses or any personal identifiers. It only provides us with completely anonymous, aggregated data showing which countries our visitors originate from and what devices/browsers they use. Because no personal data is tracked, no cookie consent banner is legally required under the GDPR or ePrivacy directive.
privacy.enS4: Our digital infrastructure consists of two parts:
privacy.enS4Li1: Frontend Hosting (GitHub Pages): Our static website files are hosted on GitHub Pages (GitHub, Inc., USA). GitHub automatically collects standard server log files (including your IP address, browser type, and time of access) to ensure security and stability based on Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR.
privacy.enS4Li2: Backend Server (Railway): Our computation server is hosted by Railway (Railway Corp., USA) deployed in their Singapore data center. This server processes your connection data (IP address) and computation inputs strictly transiently in-memory for the duration of your request based on Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR (performance of a service). Data transfer to Singapore is governed by standard contractual clauses provided by the infrastructure provider.
privacy.enS5: To save your configuration preferences directly on your device between sessions, this website uses your browser’s “localStorage”. This data stays strictly on your device. Since this storage is technically necessary to provide the service you explicitly requested, it complies with § 25 Abs. 2 TDDDG / ePrivacy Directive. You can delete it anytime via your browser settings.
privacy.enS6: To display real-time prices, this website fetches data from public APIs (such as Binance). When your browser connects to these APIs, your IP address is processed by the respective provider to deliver the market prices. No portfolio data is transmitted during this process.
privacy.enS7P1: Disclaimer: This service is provided “as is” without warranties of any kind. All financial decisions remain the responsibility of the user. No liability is assumed for losses or damages resulting from the use of this service.
privacy.enS7P2: Legacy Policy: In the event of discontinuation, abandonment, or shutdown of this service, no user data will remain on our infrastructure, as nothing is ever logged or stored server-side. Any portfolio configurations or preferences saved in your browser’s localStorage remain solely on your own device. Users retain full control of this data locally and can erase it at any moment by clearing their browser.
privacy.enS8: Since we do not store any personal data or portfolio data on our servers, we cannot provide information or delete data — we simply do not possess it. For questions regarding the transient processing of your connection data, you can contact us at momo.demirov@gmail.com.
